List all assumptions underpinning your pricing across all three sections above. Include: FTE volume assumptions, indexation methodology, how you have structured fixed vs. variable components, your fixed fee breakdown (front-office vs. back-office), any vendor-funded technology investment or implementation credit, and any deal sweeteners, transition credits, or gain-share mechanisms proposed.
All agent rates are billed per Productive Hour.

Ubiquity will provide a 10.0% discount on productive hour rates for new agent training, up to the following maximum training periods:

• Customer Service: 2 weeks

• Fraud/Disputes and Back Office: 4 weeks is expected, but is subject to further due diligence post-LOI to determine and finalize

• Debt & Collections: 2 weeks
Financial summary is based on current headcount provide in Appendix D. Revenue for EMEA is based on Live Agent rate. YOY rate increase is set to 3%
Non-USD converted based on current market rates on March 26, 2026.

Projected costs are based on current servicing location. Ubiquity will review and potentially adjust agent locations during any projected year. This will impact projected cost projections. These are not calculated in the cost, but are factored into the savings %.

Payments listed in 6.4 are suggested payments based on observed best practices. Ubiquity is open to working with FIS to determine the best course of action and will adjust the terms if needed to address FIS' business needs.

Summarize the total cost of the proposed engagement over the contract term. All figures in USD millions. This table will be used to calculate Total Contract Value and net savings versus FIS's cost baseline. Figures must be consistent with pricing submitted in Sections 6.1–6.3 above. Efficiency savings figures in this table must reconcile directly to commitments stated in Tab 5.0 Technology Solution. Any discrepancy between this table and Tab 5.0 must be explained in Section 6.4 Pricing Assumptions.
